technicalRecon.subtitle: BAMTech private REST/GraphQL API, OAuth device grant flow, and bamgrid.com infrastructure

apiArchitecture.title: BAMTech Private API (REST + GraphQL)
apiArchitecture.description: Disney+ is built on BAMTech (Disney Streaming) infrastructure, originally spun off from MLB Advanced Media. The primary API uses a combination of REST endpoints and GraphQL served from disney.api.edge.bamgrid.com. Authentication flows through a device registration system at global.edge.bamgrid.com, which issues OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens used for all subsequent requests. Two independent community reverse-engineering projects (jonbarrow/disneyplus-client in Node.js, pam-param-pam/Disney-Plus-api-wrapper in Python) have successfully implemented authentication, profile listing, and content metadata retrieval against these private endpoints.

codeExample.exampleComment: // Step 1: Device grant, Step 2: Token exchange, Step 3: Login with credentials

endpoints.purpose: OAuth token exchange (device grant, login, refresh)
Primary GraphQL gateway for profile, content, and account operations

authDetails.label: Primary Auth
authDetails.description: JWT access token obtained via device grant flow at global.edge.bamgrid.com/token. Short-lived (~30 min TTL) with refresh token rotation.

authDetails.label: Device Registration
authDetails.description: Device must be registered via /devices endpoint before token exchange. Returns device assertion used in OAuth flow.

authDetails.label: API Key
authDetails.description: Client-specific API key embedded in the web application bundle. Required for initial device registration.

authDetails.label: Profile Context
authDetails.description: GraphQL queries scoped to active profile via profile ID parameter in query variables.

authDetails.label: Refresh Token
authDetails.description: Long-lived refresh token used to obtain new access tokens without re-authentication. Stored by community clients for session persistence.

rateLimitNotes: JWT access tokens have short TTL (~30 minutes) requiring frequent refresh via refresh token
BAMTech infrastructure uses AWS-based WAF and CDN edge protection via Akamai
Device fingerprinting via SDK configuration and device assertion tokens
Paid sharing crackdown monitors device links and IP addresses for household enforcement
No explicit rate limiting documented, but aggressive token rotation acts as implicit throttle
Two independent community API clients have operated without reported blocking
Disney has historically enforced ToS against scraping services (e.g., Disney Dining reservation scrapers)
technicalRecon.harCaptureCount: GraphQL gateway consolidates most operations into single endpoint; 6 key endpoint categories identified

tosWarning.title: Disney Terms of Use — Section 2.B.x Automated Access Prohibition
tosWarning.description: Disney Terms of Use Section 2.B.x explicitly prohibits accessing, monitoring, or copying Disney Products using robots, spiders, scripts, or other automated means. Section 2.B.v prohibits reverse engineering. Disney paid a $10M COPPA settlement in December 2025, indicating heightened regulatory sensitivity. Paid sharing crackdown infrastructure actively monitors for out-of-household access. Account suspension or termination is possible if automation is detected.

overallRisk.description: BAMTech infrastructure uses AWS WAF protections. Paid sharing crackdown adds IP-based detection. COPPA settlement creates heightened sensitivity to child data access. Disney has enforcement precedent against scrapers.

detectionVectors.mitigation: Register a persistent device assertion and reuse across sessions to avoid new-device triggers
Follow standard refresh token rotation flow; do not reuse expired tokens
Use Playwright stealth plugin for all browser-automated write operations
Add human-like delays (3-8s) between operations; avoid rapid sequential mutations
Server-based access from non-household IPs may trigger sharing verification. Consider residential proxy or browser extension approach.
Mirror exact query structures from production web client; avoid custom queries

mitigationsChecklist: Register persistent device assertion and cache across sessions
Implement proper JWT refresh token rotation with standard timing
Use Playwright stealth mode for all browser-automated write operations
Cache API read responses to minimize request frequency
Add human-like random delays (3-8s) between navigation and mutation steps
Mirror exact API request structures from the production web/mobile client
Monitor for authentication failures and implement exponential backoff
Batch parental control changes into single authenticated sessions
Mitigate household detection by minimizing server-side access frequency

stats.label: Phosra-Added
stats.color: bg-green-100 text-green-700 dark:bg-green-900/30 dark:text-green-300 ring-1 ring-green-300 dark:ring-green-700

gapOpportunities.title: Screen Time Limits
gapOpportunities.gap: Disney+ has ZERO native screen time limits. No way for parents to restrict daily or weekly viewing hours within the app.
gapOpportunities.solution: Phosra monitors viewing activity via API polling. When the daily limit is reached, the child's profile is locked by setting a Phosra-managed PIN via Playwright automation.

gapOpportunities.title: Activity Alerts
gapOpportunities.gap: Disney+ sends no real-time alerts to parents. Parents must manually switch to the child's profile and browse Continue Watching to see what was viewed.
gapOpportunities.solution: Phosra polls Continue Watching data and pushes real-time notifications to parents when new content appears or viewing patterns change.

gapOpportunities.title: Bedtime Mode
gapOpportunities.gap: Disney+ has no scheduling or time-of-day restrictions. Children can watch content at any hour without limits.
gapOpportunities.solution: Phosra locks and unlocks profiles on a parent-defined schedule via cron-triggered Playwright automation. Profile PIN is toggled to enforce bedtime windows.

gapOpportunities.title: Usage Reports
gapOpportunities.gap: Disney+ provides no parental dashboard or usage analytics. Continue Watching exists but has no duration data, no timestamps, no aggregation, and no export capability.
gapOpportunities.solution: Phosra aggregates Continue Watching data into weekly/monthly reports with activity estimates and content breakdowns delivered to parents.

steps.title: Monitor
steps.detail: Poll Continue Watching data every 30 min via BAMTech API using cached JWT access tokens with automatic refresh

steps.title: Detect
steps.detail: Detect new titles appearing in Continue Watching and estimate activity level vs parent-set limit (note: no duration data available from Disney+)

steps.title: Lock
steps.detail: Set a Phosra-managed PIN on the child's profile via Playwright automation, requiring account password for changes

steps.title: Notify
steps.detail: Push real-time notification to parent via Phosra app with activity summary and lock confirmation

limitations.title: ~30 min polling delay
limitations.description: Viewing activity is polled, not streamed in real-time. Children may overshoot the limit by up to 30 minutes before the profile is locked.

limitations.title: No public Disney+ API
limitations.description: All write operations require Playwright browser automation. JWT access tokens expire every ~30 minutes and must be refreshed via the OAuth flow.

limitations.title: Account password required for PIN changes
limitations.description: Disney+ requires the account password (not just a PIN) to add or modify a profile PIN, adding complexity to automated lock/unlock cycles but providing no MFA challenge.

limitations.title: Household detection risk
limitations.description: Disney+ paid sharing crackdown monitors IP addresses. Phosra server access from non-household IPs may trigger sharing verification prompts.

enforcementFlow.loopLabel: Repeats daily until parent adjusts limits

methods.name: authenticate
methods.recommendation: Use BAMTech device grant flow: register device, exchange credentials for JWT. Cache refresh token for session persistence.

methods.name: listProfiles
methods.recommendation: Query account profiles via API. Returns profile IDs, names, maturity settings, and Junior Mode status.

methods.name: getProfileSettings
methods.recommendation: API for basic metadata (rating, Junior Mode, PIN status). Playwright scrape for full settings (autoplay, Kid-Proof Exit).

methods.name: setContentRestrictions
methods.recommendation: Playwright to navigate profile edit UI and adjust content rating. Requires account password. Simpler than Netflix MFA.

methods.name: setTitleRestrictions
methods.recommendation: Disney+ does not offer per-title blocking. Return UnsupportedOperationError.

methods.name: setProfilePIN
methods.recommendation: Playwright to set/change profile PIN. Requires account password. Primary mechanism for screen time enforcement.

methods.name: getWatchHistory
methods.recommendation: API + Playwright scrape of Continue Watching. No duration data. Significantly less data than Netflix.

methods.name: lockProfile
methods.recommendation: Set Phosra-managed PIN on child profile via Playwright. Requires account password. Primary screen time enforcement.

methods.name: createKidsProfile
methods.recommendation: Playwright to create profile with Junior Mode enabled. Verify profile count (max 7). May require account password if profile creation is restricted.

methods.name: supportsNativeScreenTime
methods.recommendation: Returns false. Disney+ has zero native screen time features.

timelinePhases.label: Foundation
timelinePhases.components: Session Manager (2-3d), Read Layer via API (1-2d)

timelinePhases.label: Enforcement
timelinePhases.components: Write Layer via Playwright (3-4d), Household Mitigation (1-2d), Testing (2-3d)
